title: Hazelnut Mochi with Portuguese Custard
description: A unique dessert that combines the flavors of Japan with the sweetness of Portugal.
instructions: 1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the mochi rice flour and granulated sugar.
2. Gradually add in the chopped hazelnuts and mix until well combined.
3. In a separate saucepan, heat the Portuguese custard over low heat.
4. Add in the unsalted butter and vanilla extract, and mix until well combined.
5. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the custard has thickened.
6. In a separate saucepan, bring the water to a boil.
7. Gradually add in the mochi rice flour mixture, and mix until a dough forms.
8. Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes, or until it becomes smooth and elastic.
9. Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces, and shape each piece into a ball.
10. Flatten each ball of dough into a disk shape, and place a tablespoon of the Portuguese custard in the center of each disk.
11. Fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle, and press the edges together to seal the mochi.
12. Repeat with the remaining dough and filling, and serve warm.
13. Drizzle with additional Portuguese custard, if desired.
